comments.text: There's an "exponnt" that's missing an e.
Massive improvement on the lower bound today! The website/problem has already silently been updated, so this comment is just to emphasize the importance & not let it go unnoticed.

In the comments on the June 11 update of the paper (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28793), Bradač gives the following, even more exciting, news "A forthcoming paper will supersede this work. The main theorem will be improved to the optimal bound $r(s,k)\geq k^{s-1+o(1)}$"
It seems to already be out on his website, though it's a rough version and has a few typos https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Y-DkeWUOXspgxj8o7USmzThEaapZw00B/view?usp=drivesdk
He has updated the arXiv version with the new bound, solving this problem: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.28793v3 (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
The Bradač paper has a complete Lean formalization linked here: https://www.math.cmu.edu/~wes/trellis.php
